reasoning_content: We need to analyze the mutation. Original: `int grid_size = (size + block_size - 1) / block_size;` Mutated: `int grid_size = (size + block_size - 1) / block_size - 1;` So grid_size is reduced by 1. This grid_size is used only in the else branch (when not taking vectorized path). The input shape is fixed [16,16384] => size = 262144. That satisfies size >= 4096 and size % 4 == 0 (262144%4=0). So the vectorized path is always taken: `if (size >= 4096 && (size % 4) == 0) { ... }` Therefore the mutated grid_size is never used. The vectorized kernel uses `vectorized_grid` which is computed separately and unchanged. So the mutation has no effect. The mutant is unkillable under fixed shape contract because the mutation is dead code. Need to confirm that the else branch is never entered. Also note that the vectorized path uses a different grid_size (vectorized_grid) which is computed from (size/4 + block_size -1)/block_size and capped. The mutation only affects the non-vectorized path. Since the shape always triggers vectorized path, the mutation is unreachable. Therefore killable=false. Reason category: predicate_unreachable. Proof sketch: size=262144, satisfies both conditions for vectorized path, so else branch never taken. Survival reason: The mutation subtracts 1 from grid_size used only in the non-vectorized kernel; fixed shape always takes vectorized path, so mutation has no effect. Killable false. Suggested test null. Recommendations: ensure launch configuration mutations are covered by testing paths that actually use the mutated variable.
